column1: Sentence
BOT REQUIREMENTS
1. Communication channel and protocol
- use of protocols open by default in corporate networks: HTTPS, DNS, ICMP
- communication channel redundancy
- server verification (verification by digital signature or some other way to make sure that it is not a control hijacking)
- search for a command server in conditions of failure of all known addresses (preferably)
- additional encryption of traffic as protection against machine analysis
- an easy to parsing protocol.
I mean, not json/xml/etc - why do we need complex parsers if we can do without them?
- extensible protocol.
Ease of adding commands
2. State (stateless-bot)
- It is impossible to store such things as configs, IDs on disk, neither in files, nor in the registry, nowhere.
That is, the bot must be "reentrant" - able to start from scratch, generate its id in a repeatable way,
look around in the system and continue working, regardless of the previous state of itself and the admin area.
- hence the requirement to the protocol - minimize blocking requests, provide a way to reset such requests.
For example: if we gave a command to the bot, and at that moment the machine rebooted, the admin will wait forever for a response.
And vice versa, if the bot expects something from the server, and the connection is broken, there must be a way to get out of such a clash.
3. Fastening
- it is desirable to duplicate the methods of fixing
- it is desirable to use steganography for anchoring.
For example, we fix not the bot itself, but this script:
@echo off
REM get the loader from the "certificate"
certutil -decode file.crt file.exe
REM run the loader
file.exe
REM wait for the file to load and work.
REM delete the loader, since it has already had time to work and start the load.
del /f /y file.exe
That is, we use standard OS utilities for unpacking and launching the load, placing harmless scripts in plain sight.
You can also use storage in a picture, sound file, etc. - just as long as there is a way to deploy the load using only standard tools.
- It is desirable to separate the bot into a loader and a payload
4. Protective measures
At least protection from machine analysis is mandatory.
- obfuscation of strings and system calls using modern automated methods.
- if you need to encrypt something, use simple means - XOR gamming or XOR 1 byte.
- sandboxing checks are not needed because it is hard to come up with a good logic for it.
- anti-debugging can be done
- use hook removal, injection protection, mitigations, to make debugging and analyzing the process more difficult.
- if possible, protection against process stripping (like AB does)
- you can use an LLVM obfuscator for builds
5. Functionality
- only fileless techniques, for everything.
No writing anything to disk
- cmd-shell (mandatory)
- powershell shell (preferably)
- executing arbitrary shell code https://github.com/DimopoulosElias/SimpleShellcodeInjector/blob/master/SimpleShellcodeInjector.c
- executing an .exe file (preferably)
- running a .dll (rundll, regsvr) is desirable
Botnet separation
- the bot must contain a botnet identifier in case multiple botnets work through one admin area
7. Auto-update
- the bot must provide for auto-updating
